channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Kennedy Center president departs – months before the art complex's scheduled closing
item.description: In a post on Truth Social, President Trump announced Friday afternoon that Richard Grenell is leaving the Kennedy Center. The arts complex is scheduled to close in July for renovations.
item.content:encoded:
Melania at the Kennedy Center in Washington, DC, on January 29, 2026.'/>

In a post on Truth Social, President Trump announced Friday afternoon that Richard Grenell is leaving the Kennedy Center. The arts complex is scheduled to close in July for renovations.

(Image credit: ALEX WROBLEWSKI/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: And the Oscar goes to — wait, why is it called an Oscar?
item.description: The Academy Awards officially adopted the "Oscars" nickname in 1939. But who is Oscar, and who started calling them that? We may never know. But here are four enduring legends to consider.
item.content:encoded:
The Academy Awards officially adopted the "Oscars" nickname in 1939. But who is Oscar, and who started calling them that? We may never know. But here are four enduring legends to consider.

(Image credit: Matt Sayles)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: 'Scarpetta' is a captivating murder mystery — and a high-wire balancing act
item.description: Based on a series of novels by best-selling author Patricia Cornwell, Scarpetta follows two different mysteries from two different timelines. It's structurally complicated — but it all holds up.
item.content:encoded: Based on a series of novels by best-selling author Patricia Cornwell, Scarpetta follows two different mysteries from two different timelines. It's structurally complicated — but it all holds up.
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli

item.title: 'Derry Girls' creator returns with a gleeful riff on the murder mystery
item.description: In the hilarious Netflix series How to Get to Heaven from Belfast, three women learn that a long estranged school friend has died in a suspicious manner — and take it upon themselves to investigate.
item.content:encoded:
In the hilarious Netflix series How to Get to Heaven from Belfast, three women learn that a long estranged school friend has died in a suspicious manner — and take it upon themselves to investigate.

(Image credit: Christopher Barr)
item.dc:creator: John Powers

item.title: Giant robots battle it out in Detroit's Robowar
item.description: Fighting robots is a cultural fantasy going back at least to Richard Matheson's 1956 story "Steel." One Detroit impresario is now bringing the idea to the stage — and real audiences.
item.content:encoded:
Fighting robots is a cultural fantasy going back at least to Richard Matheson's 1956 story "Steel." One Detroit impresario is now bringing the idea to the stage — and real audiences.

(Image credit: Timothy Chen Allen)
item.dc:creator: Neda Ulaby

item.title: Why 'Sinners' should win best picture (but probably won't) — and more Oscar predictions
item.description: NPR critics share their hopes and predictions for the 2026 Academy Awards, which air on Sunday.
item.content:encoded:
NPR critics share their hopes and predictions for the 2026 Academy Awards, which air on Sunday.

(Image credit: Richard Harbaugh/AMPAS)
item.dc:creator: Linda Holmes

item.title: Benicio del Toro reflects on the acting class that changed his life
item.description: One of del Toro's early acting teachers taught him to understand his character before learning lines. He's up for an Oscar for his role in One Battle After Another. Originally broadcast June 12, 2025.
item.content:encoded: One of del Toro's early acting teachers taught him to understand his character before learning lines. He's up for an Oscar for his role in One Battle After Another. Originally broadcast June 12, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Comedian Chris Fleming loves Terry Gross
item.description: In the new HBO special from Chris Fleming, the comedian spends a lot of time talking about Terry Gross. Wild Card host Rachel Martin talks to Fleming about what he loves about the Fresh Air host.
item.content:encoded: In the new HBO special from Chris Fleming, the comedian spends a lot of time talking about Terry Gross. Wild Card host Rachel Martin talks to Fleming about what he loves about the Fresh Air host.

item.title: This tale of a Chicago school book ban was inspired by true events
item.description: Librarian Jarrett Dapier's graphic novel tells a fictionalized account of real-life events in 2013 that restricted access to Marjane Satrapi's memoir Persepolis in Chicago Public Schools.
item.content:encoded: Librarian Jarrett Dapier's graphic novel tells a fictionalized account of real-life events in 2013 that restricted access to Marjane Satrapi's memoir Persepolis in Chicago Public Schools.
item.dc:creator: Tahneer Oksman

item.title: Chilean Smiljan Radić Clarke wins architecture's highest honor
item.description: The Pritzker Prize was awarded Thursday. "In every work, he is able to answer with radical originality, making the unobvious obvious," said fellow Chilean architect and prize chair Alejandro Aravena.
item.content:encoded:
The Pritzker Prize was awarded Thursday. "In every work, he is able to answer with radical originality, making the unobvious obvious," said fellow Chilean architect and prize chair Alejandro Aravena.

(Image credit: Iwan Baan)
item.dc:creator: Neda Ulaby
